html:
“Needs fertilization if transplanted.”

-for Grass

“This needs some fertilizing after planting.”

-for Grass Tuft

Grass Tufts are Plants commonly found in Savanna and Grasslands, but they can also appear in other biomes. They can also be found in a few underground biomes, notably the Sunken Forest.

Grass can be harvested to receive 1 Cut Grass, after which they will need three days to regrow (Rain will reduce this time).

Grasscan be dug up with a Shovel or Regal Shovel and replanted in a new location (typically closer to a base camp). Doing this will give it a shorter, withered appearance, requiring the tuft to be fertilized before it will regrow any new grass. It must be fertilized again after 20 harvests. Grass will not grow during Winter. Grass Tufts can't be planted on Checkerboard Floorings, Wooden Floorings, Cobblestones, Carpeted Floorings, Rocky Turfs, Sandy Turfs, and Cave Rock Turfs, unless it has been planted before these Turfs were placed.

These plants are flammable and can be used as long-lasting fuel in Campfires and Fire Pits, but that is not recommended since Grass Tufts are a nonrenewable resource. When unpicked Grass Tufts are burned, they will leave behind 1 Ash and no plantable tuft. The same applies to dug-up tufts.

Because they burn easily and are usually planted very close together in a large cluster, Grass Tufts are very susceptible to Lightning strikes which occur during heavy Rain. This can be prevented by building Lightning Rods near them.

Grass Tufts will turn into Withered Grass Tufts (with a similar appearance as those that need fertilization) during Summer. During this condition, Grass Tufts are unharvestable for their Grass, and digging these will yield 1 Cut Grass and no replantable Tuft. A way of curing or preventing these from withering is to simply apply fertilizers or build an Ice Flingomatic nearby.

Grass will sometimes transform into living Grass Gekkos when being picked.

Grass are renewable in Endless and Wilderness Game Modes through Resource Renewal.

Trivia

  • Grass Tufts were worth 20 research points before that feature was removed.
  • Although Grass Tufts in Hamlet are Green, Grass appears Yellow.

Gallery

  • A Grass Tuft in game.
  • Fertilized and Withered Grass Tufts.
  • Guardian Pigs guarding patches of Grass.
  • A Savanna with several Grass Tufts, as seen on the Map.
  • A Diseased Grass Tuft in Don't Starve Together.
  • A picked Diseased Grass Tuft in Don't Starve Together.
  • Unused Burnt Model
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • On Concept Art from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.

You do tend to find a lot of it outside. |choose = wolfgang}} {{Scrapbook Info|Needs fertilization if transplanted.|for Grass}} {{Scrapbook Info|This needs some fertilizing after planting.|for Grass Tuft}} '''Grass Tufts''' are [[Plants]] commonly found in [[Savanna]] and [[Grasslands]], but they can also appear in other [[biome]]s. They can also be found in a few [[Caves|underground]] biomes, notably the [[Sunken Forest]]. Grass can be harvested to receive 1 [[Cut Grass]], after which they will need three days to regrow ([[Rain]] will reduce this time). Grasscan be dug up with a [[Shovel]] or Regal Shovel and replanted in a new location (typically closer to a base camp). Doing this will give it a shorter, withered appearance, requiring the tuft to be [[Fertilizer|fertilized]] before it will regrow any new grass. It must be fertilized again after 20 harvests. Grass will not grow during [[Winter]]. Grass Tufts can't be planted on [[Checkerboard Flooring]]s, [[Wooden Flooring]]s, [[Cobblestone]]s, [[Carpeted Flooring]]s, [[Rocky Turf]]s, [[Sandy Turf]]s, and [[Cave Rock Turf]]s, unless it has been planted before these [[Turf]]s were placed. These plants are flammable and can be used as long-lasting fuel in [[Campfires]] and Fire Pits, but that is not recommended since Grass Tufts are a nonrenewable resource. When unpicked Grass Tufts are burned, they will leave behind 1 [[Ashes|Ash]] and no plantable tuft. The same applies to dug-up tufts. Because they burn easily and are usually planted very close together in a large cluster, Grass Tufts are very susceptible to [[Lightning]] strikes which occur during heavy Rain. This can be prevented by building [[Lightning Rod]]s near them. Grass Tufts will turn into Withered Grass Tufts (with a similar appearance as those that need fertilization) during [[Summer]]. During this condition, Grass Tufts are unharvestable for their Grass, and digging these will yield 1 Cut Grass and no replantable Tuft. A way of curing or preventing these from withering is to simply apply fertilizers or build an [[Ice Flingomatic]] nearby. Grass will sometimes transform into living [[Grass Gekko]]s when being picked.
